findings.title: source_file comma delimiter breaks gate — pipe required
findings.description: workflow-gate.sh splits patterns.source_file using IFS='|' but config has '*.sh,*.md' (comma). The entire string is one glob that never matches, so classify_file() always returns 'other' for .sh and .md files. PAT-003 gate is completely non-functional for this project.
findings.evidence: Config line 17: '*.sh,*.md'. Gate line 287: IFS='|'. All setup-generated configs use pipe: '*.ts|*.tsx'.
findings.impact: Phase-gated file operations (PAT-003) are broken for all source files in this project.
findings.instance_fix: Change '*.sh,*.md' to '*.sh|*.md' in both config files
findings.class_fix: Add config validation in setup rejecting commas in pattern fields. Add test.

findings.title: cpr-review triggers high+ checks at standard intensity; documents invalid 'low' level
findings.description: cpr-review/SKILL.md condition 'if workflow.intensity is set (any value)' fires at standard intensity. Also documents 'low' as valid level which doesn't exist.
findings.evidence: skills/cpr-review/SKILL.md line 242 vs workflow-advance.sh line 689-691 (only standard|high|critical).
findings.impact: Standard-intensity projects get high-intensity review checks. 'low' in docs confuses configuration.
findings.instance_fix: Replace with canonical effective intensity check. Remove 'low'.
findings.class_fix: Test asserting all skills use only standard/high/critical.

findings.title: workflow-gate.sh branch_slug() fails open in detached HEAD
findings.description: Gate's branch_slug() has no empty-branch guard. In detached HEAD, slug is empty, state file path is 'workflow-state--d41d8c.json' (never exists), gate exits 0 (allow all). workflow-advance.sh has the guard; gate copy does not.
findings.evidence: workflow-gate.sh:60-67 has no '[ -n "$branch" ]' check. workflow-advance.sh:27-37 does.
findings.impact: All phase gating bypassed in detached HEAD state.
findings.instance_fix: Add empty-branch guard matching workflow-advance.sh's version.
findings.class_fix: Extract branch_slug() into shared library to prevent drift.

findings.title: write_state() orphans .$$  temp file when jq fails
findings.description: write_state() creates $sf.$$ before jq runs. If jq fails (bad JSON), set -e aborts, no trap cleans up the temp file. 4 sites affected across workflow-advance.sh and workflow-gate.sh.
findings.evidence: workflow-advance.sh:60, 726, 825-826. workflow-gate.sh:131-132. No trap in either file.
findings.impact: Temp files accumulate in .correctless/artifacts/ on any state corruption event.
findings.instance_fix: Add trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT around write_state temp file usage.
findings.class_fix: Top-level EXIT trap in each hook script cleaning registered temp files.

findings.title: marketplace.json fix broke test R-003 — expects stale './correctless'
findings.description: Commit 1926817 changed source from './correctless' to '../correctless' but test-dynamic-rigor.sh R-003 still asserts the old value. Test fails on every run.
findings.evidence: test-dynamic-rigor.sh:211 expects './correctless'. marketplace.json has '../correctless'.
findings.impact: Failing test in CI. Blocks all future merges that run this suite.
findings.instance_fix: Change expected value from './correctless' to '../correctless'.
findings.class_fix: Grep tests for old values before closing fix commits.

findings.title: spec-full.md and spec-lite.md missing from distribution — sync.sh omission
findings.description: templates/spec-full.md and spec-lite.md exist in source but are not in sync.sh's template list. Distribution correctless/templates/ is missing both. sync.sh --check gives false negative.
findings.evidence: diff -rq templates/ correctless/templates/ shows both files missing. sync.sh line 63 omits them.
findings.impact: /cspec cannot load spec templates from installed distribution. Affects all intensity levels.
findings.instance_fix: Add spec-full.md and spec-lite.md to sync.sh template list. Run sync.
findings.class_fix: sync.sh --check should detect source files not in distribution. Add reverse check.

findings.title: Package key injected unquoted into jq expression — crashes gate on special chars
findings.description: workflow-gate.sh line 225 interpolates $key into jq filter string. A key with '"' breaks jq syntax, gate exits code 3, likely failing open.
findings.evidence: jq -r ".packages[\"$key\"].path" — shell interpolation, not --arg.
findings.impact: Malformed monorepo config key disables gate. Low probability but trivial fix.
findings.instance_fix: Use jq --arg k "$key" '.packages[$k].path'.
findings.class_fix: Never interpolate shell vars into jq filter strings. Use --arg.

findings.title: run_tests() is dead code with unchecked eval
findings.description: run_tests() defined at lines 182-209 but never called. tests_pass() and tests_fail_not_build_error() implement the same logic independently.
findings.evidence: Zero call sites for run_tests() in the file.
findings.impact: Maintenance trap — fixes to run_tests() have no effect.
findings.instance_fix: Remove run_tests() entirely.
findings.class_fix: Periodic dead-code audit.

findings.title: cmd_qa double-write: interrupted mid-sequence over-counts qa_rounds
findings.description: cmd_qa writes qa_rounds+1 then calls update_phase in a second write. Kill between the two writes leaves qa_rounds incremented with phase stuck in tdd-impl.
findings.evidence: workflow-advance.sh:511-515. Two write_state calls in sequence.
findings.impact: qa_rounds over-counted on retry, potentially satisfying min_qa_rounds prematurely.
findings.instance_fix: Merge both mutations into single write_state call.
findings.class_fix: Rule: one write_state per command function. Never follow write_state with update_phase.

findings.title: cmd_spec_update double-write: interrupted mid-sequence over-counts spec_updates
findings.description: Same pattern as QA-009. spec_updates incremented in first write, phase transition in second. Kill between writes leaves spec_updates over-counted.
findings.evidence: workflow-advance.sh:751-752. Two write_state calls.
findings.impact: spec_updates over-counted, prematurely triggering 3-update warning.
findings.instance_fix: Merge both mutations into single write_state call.
findings.class_fix: Same as QA-009.

findings.title: statusline.sh reads .override_remaining — field doesn't exist; should be .override.remaining_calls
findings.description: statusline.sh line 177 reads .override_remaining (flat path). State uses .override.remaining_calls (nested). Override warning never displays.
findings.evidence: statusline.sh:177 vs workflow-advance.sh:810, workflow-gate.sh:125.
findings.impact: Users never see override status in statusline. Override expires silently.
findings.instance_fix: Change to .override.remaining_calls // empty.
findings.class_fix: Document state schema in one place. All readers reference it.

findings.title: Corrupt state file JSON causes gate to exit with unspecified code — fails open
findings.description: workflow-gate.sh line 103 reads .phase with no error handling. Invalid JSON causes jq exit 5, set -e aborts with undefined hook exit code.
findings.evidence: workflow-gate.sh:103. No jq error handling, no trap.
findings.impact: Corrupt state file disables all gate protection until manually repaired.
findings.instance_fix: Wrap jq call: PHASE=$(jq ... || exit 2). Fail closed on parse error.
findings.class_fix: Gate hook should have top-level error policy (fail-open or fail-closed). Document and enforce.

findings.title: 3 test suites missing trap cleanup EXIT — leak /tmp dirs on failure
findings.description: test-dynamic-rigor.sh, test-wire-intensity-creview.sh, test-intensity-detection.sh define cleanup() but don't register trap cleanup EXIT. Other 6 test suites do.
findings.evidence: No 'trap cleanup EXIT' in those 3 files. Present in test.sh:455, test-bugfixes.sh:391, etc.
findings.impact: Failed test runs leave full repo copies in /tmp. Accumulates GB over time.
findings.instance_fix: Add trap cleanup EXIT after cleanup() definition in each file.
findings.class_fix: Test template with mandatory trap. CI check: grep -L 'trap cleanup EXIT' tests/test-*.sh.

findings.title: ARCHITECTURE.md documents wrong config path
findings.description: ARCHITECTURE.md line 53: '.claude/workflow-config.json'. Actual path: '.correctless/config/workflow-config.json'.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:53 vs workflow-advance.sh:13 and workflow-gate.sh:20.
findings.impact: Developers editing wrong config file see no effect.
findings.instance_fix: Change to .correctless/config/workflow-config.json.
findings.class_fix: Test asserting ARCHITECTURE.md config path matches hook CONFIG_FILE value.

findings.title: test-cexplain.sh missing from commands.test — never run
findings.description: 14 test files exist but commands.test chains only 13. test-cexplain.sh is excluded.
findings.evidence: Glob: 14 files. Config commands.test: 13 entries, no test-cexplain.sh.
findings.impact: /cexplain skill contracts untested by CI. Regressions pass undetected.
findings.instance_fix: Append '&& bash tests/test-cexplain.sh' to commands.test in both configs.
findings.class_fix: Meta-test: glob test files, assert all present in commands.test.

findings.title: test-cexplain.sh asserts cexplain appears 2+ times in sync.sh — appears once
findings.description: Test expects 'at least twice (once per distribution)' but sync.sh unified loop has cexplain once. Test will fail when added to CI.
findings.evidence: test-cexplain.sh:114-120 asserts >= 2. sync.sh line 74: 1 occurrence.
findings.impact: Adding test-cexplain.sh to CI (QA-015 fix) will immediately fail.
findings.instance_fix: Change assertion to >= 1.
findings.class_fix: Count-based assertions should document the architectural assumption.

findings.title: PAT-005 describes different patterns in ARCHITECTURE.md vs AGENT_CONTEXT.md
findings.description: ARCHITECTURE.md: PAT-005 = Skill Frontmatter Contract. AGENT_CONTEXT.md: PAT-005 = Effective Intensity. Same ID, different meaning.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:44 vs AGENT_CONTEXT.md:28.
findings.impact: Any citation of PAT-005 is ambiguous. 15+ skills cite PAT-005 for intensity.
findings.instance_fix: Renumber ARCHITECTURE.md frontmatter contract to PAT-006. Keep PAT-005 = effective intensity.
findings.class_fix: Meta-test: no duplicate PAT-NNN IDs across ARCHITECTURE.md and AGENT_CONTEXT.md.

findings.title: Gate exits undocumented code if mv fails during override decrement
findings.description: workflow-gate.sh:128-134 override decrement uses set -e with && chain. mv failure exits code 1 (not 0 or 2). Claude Code receives undefined exit code.
findings.evidence: workflow-gate.sh:128-134. No error handling around mv.
findings.impact: Disk-full during override causes undefined gate behavior for one tool call.
findings.instance_fix: Add || rm -f cleanup. Always reach exit 0.
findings.class_fix: Gate hook must only exit 0 or 2. Top-level trap 'exit 0' ERR for fail-open.

findings.title: detect_affected_packages() uses unescaped regex in grep — regex metachar in path misclassifies
findings.description: workflow-advance.sh:156 uses grep -q with $pkg_path as regex. Paths with [, ., + are interpreted as regex metacharacters.
findings.evidence: workflow-advance.sh:156. grep -q "^$pkg_path/".
findings.impact: Wrong package selected for test execution in monorepo workflows.
findings.instance_fix: Use grep -qF for fixed-string match.
findings.class_fix: Config-derived values passed to grep should use -F unless regex is intended.

findings.title: .pkg-cache files accumulate without bound — old mtime-keyed files never deleted
findings.description: workflow-gate.sh:213 writes .pkg-cache-${mtime}.json. New config edits create new files; old ones never cleaned up. cmd_reset doesn't remove them.
findings.evidence: workflow-gate.sh:213. No cleanup anywhere for .pkg-cache-*.json.
findings.impact: Stale cache files accumulate indefinitely. Small but unbounded.
findings.instance_fix: Delete old .pkg-cache-*.json after writing new one. Add to cmd_reset.
findings.class_fix: Cache files must include rotation step at write time.

findings.title: .correctless/config paths section uses stale .claude/ locations
findings.description: paths.antipatterns, paths.artifacts, paths.state, paths.specs all point to old .claude/ or docs/ dirs. .claude/workflow-config.json has correct paths.
findings.evidence: .correctless/config/workflow-config.json:36-43 vs actual file locations.
findings.impact: Skills reading paths from config get wrong directory. Hooks hardcode paths so unaffected.
findings.instance_fix: Update paths to match .claude/workflow-config.json.
findings.class_fix: Eliminate dual-config. One authoritative config file.

findings.title: audit-trail.sh adherence temp file orphaned on mv failure
findings.description: audit-trail.sh:153,158 uses write-then-mv with 2>/dev/null on both. mv failure leaves temp silently.
findings.evidence: audit-trail.sh:153,158. No cleanup on mv failure.
findings.impact: Low — mv within same filesystem rarely fails.
findings.instance_fix: Add || rm -f cleanup on mv failure.
findings.class_fix: Same as QA-004 class fix.

findings.title: setup overwrites .statusLine unconditionally in partial-registration path
findings.description: setup:350 sets .statusLine unconditionally when updating existing settings.json. User-customized statusline overwritten.
findings.evidence: setup line 350: .statusLine = {command: $sl}. No if-absent guard.
findings.impact: Users with custom statusline lose it on re-run of setup.
findings.instance_fix: Use conditional: if .statusLine then . else {command: $sl}.
findings.class_fix: Idempotent installers must use set-if-absent for all user config fields.

findings.title: fmt_tokens() injects unvalidated $n into awk program string
findings.description: statusline.sh:50,52 uses awk with $n interpolated. Non-integer token value causes awk parse error.
findings.evidence: statusline.sh:50,52. No integer validation before awk.
findings.impact: Display-only failure — blank statusline on bad data.
findings.instance_fix: Add integer guard: [[ "$n" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]].
findings.class_fix: Validate jq-extracted values as numeric before arithmetic use.

findings.title: ARCHITECTURE.md says 11 test suites; actual count is 14
findings.description: ARCHITECTURE.md line 15 says '11 shell test suites' but lists 14 names. Stale count.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE.md:15 vs ls tests/test*.sh (14 files).
findings.impact: Misleading documentation. No behavioral impact.
findings.instance_fix: Change '11' to '14'.
findings.class_fix: Remove hardcoded count; say 'see tests/test*.sh'.

rejected.title: override-log.json append-only, never pruned by reset
rejected.reason: Intentional design — override events are rare audit records. Unbounded growth is acceptable.
